title: Engine assembly including cam for z-type engines
abstract: A compact and efficient Z-twin internal combustion engine is described herein. The Z-twin internal combustion engine comprises horizontally opposed cylinder arrangement that allows for vibration cancellation. The Z-twin engine comprises a central shared cam that drives angled side valves of both the opposing cylinders, thereby greatly reducing moving parts and thus provides a significantly more efficient angled valve approach.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A Z-type side valve internal combustion engine (100) comprising: (a) a primary drive gear (210) coupled to a camshaft (212), wherein a first cam (418) of the camshaft (212) is coupled to a first valve (420) of a first cylinder (222) and a second valve (421) of a second cylinder (224), and wherein a second cam (416) of the camshaft (212) is coupled to a third valve (422) of the first cylinder and a fourth valve (423) of the second cylinder, and wherein a rotation of the primary drive gear (210) alternately opens and closes the first valve (420) and the second valve (220) and opens and closes the third valve (422) and the fourth valve (423), and wherein the first valve (420) and the second valve (421) are coupled diagonally to the camshaft (212) along a first diagonal axis (Î-Î'), and wherein the third valve (423) and the fourth valve (424) are coupled diagonally to the camshaft along the first diagonal axis (CB-B'); (b) a first secondary gear (206) rotatably coupled to the primary drive gear (212), and further coupled to a first piston (226) of the first cylinder (222), the first secondary gear (206) is configured to rotate from horizontal movement of the first piston (226) within the first cylinder along a first horizontal axis (D-D'); and (c) a second secondary gear (208) rotatably coupled to the primary drive gear (212), the first secondary gear (206) and the second secondary gear (208) are coupled to diametrically opposite ends of the primary drive gear (210) along a second diagonal axis (Î-Î'), wherein the second diagonal axis (Î-Î') and the first diagonal axis (Î-Î') intersect at an angle, wherein the second secondary gear (208) is further coupled to a second piston (228) of the second cylinder (224), wherein the second secondary gear (208) is configured to rotate from horizontal movement the second piston (228) within the second cylinder along a second horizontal axis (Î-Î'), and wherein the second cylinder (224) is (i) parallel to, (ii) horizontally opposed to, and (iii) vertically offset from the first cylinder (222), wherein the first horizontal axis, the second diagonal axis, and the second horizontal axis together form a Z-shape of the internal combustion engine resulting in a more compact engine with reduced vibrations and higher efficiency. 2. The engine of claim 1 , further comprising a flywheel (412) rotatably coupled to the primary drive gear (210) on the camshaft (502). 3. The engine of claim 2, further comprising an expeller (426) coupled to the camshaft (502) on an exhaust side (404) of the engine, wherein spinning of the flywheel (412) spins the expeller (426) and provides a turbine assisted exhaust evacuation for the engine, 

 thereby decreasing an overall temperature of the engine. 4. The engine of claim 2, further comprising an impeller (414) coupled to the camshaft and flywheel (502) on an intake side (402) of the engine through a series of planetary gears (700), wherein the primary drive gear (210) is configured to rotate the flywheel in an opposite direction providing vibration cancelling counter rotational force and wherein spinning of the flywheel and camshaft spins the impeller and force feeds additional air/fuel mixture into the engine thereby supercharging the engine. 5. The engine of claim 1 , wherein the first secondary gear (206) and the second secondary gear (208) rotate in the same direction while the primary drive gear and flywheel (210) rotate in an opposite direction. 6. The engine of claim 1 , wherein each of the first cylinder and the second cylinder comprises a clover-shaped cylinder head configured to increase an air/fuel turbulence, and combustion force direction, and enhance combustion characteristics of the engine. 7. The engine of claim 1 , wherein the angle at which the second diagonal axis (Î-Î') and the first diagonal axis (Î-Î') intersect ranges from about 45 to 90 degrees. 8. The engine of claim 1 , wherein the first valve (420) and the second valve (421) are intake/exhaust side valves configured to allow intake air to enter/exhaust air to exit out from a side of each cylinder, and wherein the third valve (422) and the fourth valve (423) are exhaust/intake side valves configured to allow exhaust air to exit out/intake air to enter from a side of each cylinder. 9. The engine of claim 1 , further comprising air and oil cooling mechanisms integrated with the engine. 10. A Z-twin engine (100), comprising: (a) two horizontally opposed, vertically offset parallel cylinders; (b) a central camshaft (502) driving a first cam (418) and a second cam (416), wherein the first cam (418) is configured to operate a first set of side valves of the cylinders along a first diagonal axis (Î-Î'), wherein the second cam (416) is configured to operate a second set of side valves of the cylinders along the first diagonal axis (B- B'), and wherein the central camshaft (502) comprises a primary drive gear (210) of the engine; and (c) two counterbalancing cranks coupled to the cylinders, each crank comprising a secondary gear rotatably coupled to the primary drive gear, and wherein the secondary gears of the cylinders are diametrically opposite to each other, 

 wherein placement of the two cylinders relative to the diametrical orientation of the primary and secondary gear forms a Z-shape, which allows for vibration cancellation in the engine, and wherein the horizontally opposed cylinder position along with the counterbalancing cranks together provide for a compact, simple and efficient engine design. 1 1 . The Z-twin engine of claim 10, further comprising: (a) a flywheel (412) coupled to the primary drive gear (210) of the central camshaft (502) along an intake side (402) of the engine; and (b) an intake impeller (414) coupled to the central camshaft (502) on the intake side through a series of planetary gears (700), wherein the primary drive gear (210) is configured to rotate the flywheel in an opposite direction and wherein spinning of the flywheel spins the impeller and force feeds additional air/fuel mixture into the engine thereby supercharging the engine. 12. The Z-twin engine of claim 10, further comprising: (a) a flywheel (412) coupled to the primary drive gear of the central camshaft along an intake side of the engine; and (b) an expeller (426) coupled to the central camshaft on an exhaust side of the engine, wherein spinning of the flywheel spins the expeller and provides a turbine assisted exhaust evacuation for the engine, thereby decreasing an overall temperature of the engine and preventing exhaust contamination. 13. The Z-twin engine of claim 10, wherein each cylinder comprises a clover-shaped cylinder head configured to increase an air/fuel turbulence, and combustion force direction, and enhance combustion characteristics of the engine. 14. The Z-twin engine of claim 10, wherein the first cam (418) is configured to alternately open and close the first set of valves, and the second cam (416) is configured to alternately open and close the second set of valves. 15. The Z-twin engine of claim 10, wherein the first set of valves comprises intake side valves of the two cylinders and the second set of valves comprises exhaust side valves of the two cylinders. 16. The Z-twin engine of claim 15, wherein when the first intake side valve of the first cylinder is opened, the first exhaust side valve of the first cylinder is closed, the second intake side valve of second cylinder is closed, and the second exhaust side valve of the second cylinder is opened. 17. The Z-twin engine of claim 15, wherein when the second intake side valve of the second cylinder is opened, the second exhaust side valve of the second cylinder is closed, the first intake side valve of first cylinder is closed, and the first exhaust side valve of the first cylinder is opened. 18. A Z-type internal combustion engine (100) comprising: (a) a primary drive gear (210) coupled to a camshaft (212), wherein a first cam (418) of the camshaft (212) is coupled to a first valve (216) of a first cylinder (222) and a second valve (220) of a second cylinder (224) diagonally along a first diagonal axis (Î-Î'), and wherein a second cam (416) of the camshaft (212) is coupled to a third valve (422) of the first cylinder and a fourth valve (423) of the second cylinder along the first diagonal axis (B-B'); (b) a first secondary gear (206) rotatably coupled to the primary drive gear (210), and further coupled to a first piston (226) of the first cylinder (222), the first secondary gear configured to move with the first piston horizontally within the first cylinder along a first horizontal axis (D-D'); (c) a second secondary gear (208) rotatably coupled to the primary drive gear (210), the first secondary gear and the second secondary gear are coupled to diametrically opposite ends of the primary drive gear along a second diagonal axis, wherein the second secondary gear is further coupled to a second piston of the second cylinder, wherein the second secondary gear is configured to move with the second piston horizontally within the second cylinder along a second horizontal axis, and wherein the second cylinder is (i) parallel to, (ii) horizontally opposed to, and (iii) vertically offset from the first cylinder, wherein the first horizontal axis, the second diagonal axis, the second horizontal axis together form a Z-shape of the internal combustion engine resulting in a more compact engine with reduced vibrations and higher efficiency; (d) a flywheel (412) rotatably coupled to the primary drive gear, wherein the primary drive gear is configured to rotate the flywheel in an opposite direction, wherein a series of planetary gears is rotatably coupled to the flywheel; (e) an impeller (414) coupled to an intake side of the engine through the series of planetary gears, wherein spinning of the flywheel spins the planetary gears, which spins the impeller and provides additional air/fuel mixture into the engine thereby supercharging the engine; and (f) an expeller (426) coupled to an exhaust side of the engine, wherein spinning of the flywheel spins the expeller and provides a turbine assisted exhaust evacuation for the engine, thereby decreasing an overall temperature of the engine and decreasing 

 exhaust contamination. 19. The engine of claim 18, wherein a rotation of the primary drive gear alternately opens and closes the first valve and the second valve. 20. The engine of claim 18, wherein the second diagonal axis and the first diagonal axis intersect at an angle between 45 and 90 degrees.
